2604.08260.title: Behavior-Aware Item Modeling via Dynamic Procedural Solution Representations for Knowledge Tracing
2604.08260.paper_summary: Knowledge Tracing (KT) aims to predict learners' future performance from past interactions. While recent KT approaches have improved via learning item representations aligned with Knowledge Components, they overlook the procedural dynamics of problem solving. We propose Behavior-Aware Item Modeling (BAIM), a framework that enriches item representations by integrating dynamic procedural solution information. BAIM leverages a reasoning language model to decompose each item's solution into four problem-solving stages (i.e., understand, plan, carry out, and look back), pedagogically grounded in Polya's framework. Specifically, it derives stage-level representations from per-stage embedding trajectories, capturing latent signals beyond surface features. To reflect learner heterogeneity, BAIM adaptively routes these stage-wise representations, introducing a context-conditioned mechanism within a KT backbone, allowing different procedural stages to be emphasized for different learners. Experiments on XES3G5M and NIPS34 show that BAIM consistently outperforms strong pretraining-based baselines, achieving particularly large gains under repeated learner interactions.
2604.08260.paper_summary_zh: 知識追蹤（KT）旨在根據過去的互動預測學習者的未來表現。雖然最近的KT方法通過與知識組件對齊的學習項目表示有所改進，但它們忽略了問題解決的程序動態。我們提出了行為感知項目建模（BAIM），這是一個通過整合動態程序解決信息來豐富項目表示的框架。BAIM利用推理語言模型將每個項目的解決方案分解為四個問題解決階段（即理解、計劃、執行和回顧），這在教學上基於Polya的框架。具體而言，它從每個階段的嵌入軌跡中推導出階段級表示，捕捉超越表面特徵的潛在信號。為了反映學習者的異質性，BAIM自適應地路由這些階段級表示，在KT骨幹中引入了一種上下文條件機制，使不同的程序階段能夠對不同的學習者進行強調。在XES3G5M和NIPS34上的實驗顯示，BAIM始終優於強大的基於預訓練的基準，特別是在重複學習者互動下取得了顯著的增益。
2604.08260.author: Jun Seo et.al.
2604.08260.authors: Jun Seo, Sangwon Ryu, Heejin Do, Hyounghun Kim, Gary Geunbae Lee
